https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pul: verify() no longer runs the previously existing validations (verifyNoHtmlEntities, verifyProblematicBlanks, verifyNoDiscouragedWords, verifyMessageFormat* / verifyNotMessageFormat*, verifyUnbalancedCurlyBraces). In the diff, verify() was reduced to only verifyNoDuplicateKeys(contents) and verifySafeHtml(), which is a concrete behavior regression for the verifier tool compared to the BEFORE code.

verifySafeHtml() throws RuntimeException on IO problems (both for reading the target file and the computed English file) instead of propagating MojoExecutionException like verify() does. The diff shows `catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException("unable to read file " + file, e); }` and similarly for englishFile, which changes error handling and can crash the build with an unchecked exception.

themes/src/main/resources-community/theme/base/account/messages/messages_lt.properties: Lithuanian locale string was changed to Italian text. Diff shows `totpStep1=Installa una delle seguenti applicazioni sul tuo cellulare:` which is incorrect content for lt and will display wrong language at runtime.

themes/src/main/resources-community/theme/base/account/messages/messages_zh_CN.properties: Simplified Chinese locale string was changed to Traditional Chinese wording/characters. Diff shows `totpStep1=在您的手機上安裝以下應用程式之一：` (手機/應用程式), which is incorrect for zh_CN and will display wrong text at runtime.

Regex bug in englishFile derivation: `replaceAll("_[a-zA-Z-_]*\\.properties", "_en.properties")` uses a character class `[a-zA-Z-_]` where `-` defines a range, unintentionally matching extra characters. This can compute the wrong englishFile path and break loading the English bundle (diff shows this exact regex).

AssertEvents.isAccessTokenId() matcher has inverted logic: it returns false when the extracted shortcut equals expectedGrantShortcut, so it rejects correct token IDs. Evidence (testsuite/.../AssertEvents.java):
if (items[0].substring(3, 5).equals(expectedGrantShortcut)) return false;

RecoveryAuthnCodesUtils now sets NOM_ALGORITHM_TO_HASH to Algorithm.RS512 but still calls JavaAlgorithm.getJavaAlgorithmForHash(NOM_ALGORITHM_TO_HASH) before hashing. RS512 is a JWS signature algorithm identifier, not a hash algorithm name, so this mapping is likely invalid and can break hashing at runtime or produce incorrect hashes. Evidence: server-spi/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/utils/RecoveryAuthnCodesUtils.java: `public static final String NOM_ALGORITHM_TO_HASH = Algorithm.RS512;` and `HashUtils.hash(JavaAlgorithm.getJavaAlgorithmForHash(NOM_ALGORITHM_TO_HASH), ...)`.

RecoveryAuthnCodeInputLoginBean dereferences an Optional without checking presence, which can throw NoSuchElementException if the user has no recovery-codes credential in either federated or local storage. Evidence: services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/forms/login/freemarker/model/RecoveryAuthnCodeInputLoginBean.java: `Optional<CredentialModel> credentialModelOpt = RecoveryAuthnCodesUtils.getCredential(user);` then `RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialModel.createFromCredentialModel(credentialModelOpt.get());`.

CredentialHelper.createRecoveryCodesCredential hard-codes the credential provider id string and then uses the returned provider without null-check; if the id does not match a registered provider, this will return null and cause a NullPointerException when calling createCredential. Evidence: server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/utils/CredentialHelper.java: `var recoveryCodeCredentialProvider = session.getProvider(CredentialProvider.class, "keycloak-recovery-authn-codes");` then later `recoveryCodeCredentialProvider.createCredential(realm, user, credentialModel);`.

Profile.logUnsupportedFeatures logic regression: it now filters enabled features by matching only the *type* set derived from checkedFeatures (`checkedFeatureTypes.contains(e.getKey().getType())`) instead of checking membership in `checkedFeatures` itself. This broadens the logged set and can log features that were not part of the checkedFeatures argument, producing incorrect/misleading output. Evidence: method changed from `.filter(e -> e.getValue() && checkedFeatures.contains(e.getKey()))` to computing `checkedFeatureTypes` and filtering by type.

ClientPermissionsV2#getClientsWithPermission returns resource.getName() for granted clients, but per-client resources are looked up by client.getId() (UUID) elsewhere, making the returned identifiers inconsistent/likely wrong for callers. Evidence: getClientsWithPermission(): granted.add(resource.getName()); vs hasPermission(ClientModel,...): resourceStore.findByName(server, client.getId(), server.getId()).

ClientPermissionsV2#hasPermission(String scope) looks up a Resource by name using AdminPermissionsSchema.CLIENTS_RESOURCE_TYPE (a type label like "Clients"), which is unlikely to match the actual resource name for the resource-type resource (elsewhere obtained via AdminPermissionsSchema.SCHEMA.getResourceTypeResource). This can cause global client-scope checks to always return false. Evidence: Resource resource = resourceStore.findByName(server, AdminPermissionsSchema.CLIENTS_RESOURCE_TYPE, server.getId());

ClientPermissionsV2#hasPermission(ClientModel, scope) can throw NullPointerException if getResourceTypeResource(...) returns null: it passes null into policyStore.findByResource(server, resource) and later dereferences resource.getScopes(). Evidence: resource = AdminPermissionsSchema.SCHEMA.getResourceTypeResource(...); then authz.getStoreFactory().getPolicyStore().findByResource(server, resource) and new ResourcePermission(resource, resource.getScopes(), server).

AdminPermissions.registerListener now gates permission-disable cleanup only on ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ, but V2 uses ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ_V2; in V2-only mode the listener will not run and removed roles/clients/groups won't have permissions disabled. Evidence: registerListener wraps all event handling in if (Profile.isFeatureEnabled(Profile.Feature.ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ)) { ... }, while V2 evaluator selection is based on ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ_V2 (per candidate evidence and the diff change).

GroupPermissionsV2.canManage() grants manage when caller has only VIEW scope. Evidence: GroupPermissionsV2.java `public boolean canManage() { ... return hasPermission(null, AdminPermissionsSchema.VIEW, AdminPermissionsSchema.MANAGE); }` — including `VIEW` in a manage check is an authorization logic bug (view-only permission would satisfy canManage).

GroupPermissionsV2.getGroupIdsWithViewPermission() returns authorization Resource IDs, not GroupModel IDs, which are then used as group IDs for user filtering. Evidence: GroupPermissionsV2.java adds `groupResource.getId()` to `granted` and also calls `hasPermission(groupResource.getId(), ...)`; UsersResource/BruteForceUsersResource set `session.setAttribute(UserModel.GROUPS, groupIds)` expecting group IDs. Resource IDs are not guaranteed to equal group IDs, so filtering/permission evaluation can break.

UsersResource.searchForUser() and BruteForceUsersResource.searchForUser() unconditionally call `includeServiceAccounts.toString()`, which will throw NPE when the Boolean query param is omitted (null). Evidence: both methods contain `attributes.put(UserModel.INCLUDE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT, includeServiceAccounts.toString());` with no null guard.

GroupTest.createMultiDeleteMultiReadMulti starts a background reader thread but never joins/waits for it to terminate before asserting and returning. Evidence: `new Thread(() -> { while (!deletedAll.get()) { ... } }).start();` then after deletions `deletedAll.set(true);` immediately followed by `assertThat(caughtExceptions, Matchers.empty());` with no `join()`. This makes the test incorrect/nondeterministic because exceptions can be added after the assertion and the thread can outlive the test and interfere with subsequent tests.

GroupAdapter.getSubGroupsCount() now returns null when the delegate model is missing, changing behavior and potentially introducing NPEs in callers expecting a non-null count. Evidence in diff: replaced `return getGroupModel().getSubGroupsCount();` with `GroupModel model = modelSupplier.get(); return model == null ? null : model.getSubGroupsCount();`.
https://github.com/ai-code-review-evalua: Compilation error in UsernameForm.authenticate(): it calls isConditionalPasskeysEnabled() with no arguments (`if (context.getUser() != null && !isConditionalPasskeysEnabled())`), but the only added method in the hierarchy shown in the diff is `protected boolean isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(UserModel user)` in UsernamePasswordForm. No no-arg overload is present in the provided diff, so this is a method signature mismatch that will not compile.
https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/pull: Click option declaration is invalid: in src/sentry/consumers/__init__.py the new option is defined as click.Option(["--flusher-processes", "flusher_processes"], ...). The second entry lacks leading dashes, so Click will treat it as an invalid option string (options must look like "--name" or "-n"). Evidence: diff hunk adding click.Option(["--flusher-processes", "flusher_processes"], ...).

SpanFlusher.join() does not respect the overall timeout across multiple processes: it computes remaining_time but never uses it, and then waits in a loop per process until the global deadline. This can block up to ~timeout per process (N*timeout) rather than the requested total timeout. Evidence: in join(), remaining_time is computed then ignored; the while loop condition uses the same deadline for each process without bounding per-process wait.
https://github.com/ai-code-review-evalua: fetch_error_details pairs error_ids with nodestore results using zip(error_ids, events.values()), which can mismatch IDs to event payloads because it relies on dict value iteration order rather than mapping by node_id/event_id. Evidence: `events = nodestore.backend.get_multi(node_ids)` then `for event_id, data in zip(error_ids, events.values())`.

BrowserReportSerializer mutual-exclusivity validation uses truthiness checks on initial_data (`if self.initial_data.get("age")` / `if self.initial_data.get("timestamp")`), so `age: 0` or `timestamp: 0` will be treated as absent and allow both fields to coexist without triggering validation. Evidence: validate_timestamp/validate_age implementations in diff.

WidgetCardChart feature-flag branch renders TableWidgetVisualization with hardcoded empty `columns={[]}` and empty `tableData` (data: [], meta.fields: {}), ignoring `result.data`/`result.meta`, so flagged orgs will see an empty table regardless of query results. Evidence: chart.tsx conditional rendering block under `organization.features.includes('use-table-widget-visualization')`.

OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key applies math.floor/math.ceil to the ordered field value. In this PR it is used with order_by='-datetime' (OrganizationAuditLogsEndpoint passes order_by='-datetime'), so value will be a datetime and math.floor/ceil will raise TypeError at runtime. Evidence: organization_auditlogs.py uses paginator_cls=OptimizedCursorPaginator with order_by='-datetime'; paginator.py defines get_item_key as `value = getattr(item, self.key); return int(math.floor(value) if ... else math.ceil(value))`.

OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_result attempts to slice a Django QuerySet with a negative start when enable_advanced_features is True and cursor.offset < 0. Django QuerySet slicing does not support negative indices and will raise at runtime. Evidence: paginator.py: `if self.enable_advanced_features and cursor.offset < 0: start_offset = cursor.offset; ... results = list(queryset[start_offset:stop])`.

BasePaginator.get_result was changed to allow passing a negative start offset into queryset slicing when cursor.is_prev is True (start_offset = offset without clamping). Since Cursor.offset is just int-cast (and now explicitly documented as allowing negatives), a negative offset will cause negative QuerySet slicing and raise at runtime. Evidence: paginator.py: `start_offset = max(0, offset) if not cursor.is_prev else offset` followed by `results = list(queryset[start_offset:stop])`; cursors.py now explicitly allows negative offsets via comment and still sets `self.offset = int(offset)`.
https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/: NewResourceServer now calls s.Init(ctx) but the diff does not show any ctx defined in that function scope, which would be a compile error. Evidence: pkg/storage/unified/resource/server.go adds `err := s.Init(ctx)` at @@ -255 without showing a preceding `ctx := ...` in the diff hunk.

server.Init now starts the watcher unconditionally (removed prior gating), which can change behavior and potentially break configurations where watching should not start. Evidence: pkg/storage/unified/resource/server.go moved watcher start after search init and changed condition from `if s.initErr == nil && s.storageEnabled { s.initErr = s.initWatcher() }` to `if s.initErr == nil { s.initErr = s.initWatcher() }`.
https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/pull: MetricAlertDetectorHandler is now a subclass of StatefulDetectorHandler but does not implement required abstract members, so it is non-instantiable and will raise TypeError when used. Evidence: src/sentry/incidents/grouptype.py defines `class MetricAlertDetectorHandler(StatefulDetectorHandler[QuerySubscriptionUpdate]): pass` while src/sentry/workflow_engine/processors/detector.py declares StatefulDetectorHandler abstract with required `counter_names`, `get_dedupe_value`, `get_group_key_values`, and `build_occurrence_and_event_data`.

get_monitor_environment_context computes a modified copy of config (including substituting schedule_type with get_schedule_type_display()) but returns the original unmodified monitor_environment.monitor.config, so the substitution is never applied. Evidence (incident_occurrence.py): `config = monitor_environment.monitor.config.copy(); if "schedule_type" in config: config["schedule_type"] = ...; return { ..., "config": monitor_environment.monitor.config, ... }`.

get_failure_reason can produce an incorrect/blank message when failed_checkins is empty or contains only statuses not in HUMAN_FAILURE_STATUS_MAP: status_counts becomes empty, sum != 1, get_text_list([]) yields an empty string, and it returns `"%(problem_checkins)s check-ins detected"` with an empty substitution (e.g. " check-ins detected"). Evidence (incident_occurrence.py): status_counts filters to HUMAN_FAILURE_STATUS_MAP keys; no guard for empty before building human_status and returning the formatted string.

try_incident_threshold can raise IndexError when failure_issue_threshold > 1 but there are zero prior check-ins returned by the query (e.g., data inconsistency or missing rows): it slices/reverses to `previous_checkins`, then unconditionally does `starting_checkin = previous_checkins[0]`. Evidence (incidents.py): `previous_checkins = list(reversed(previous_checkins[:failure_issue_threshold])); ... starting_checkin = previous_checkins[0]` with no empty-check.

AssignmentSource dataclass uses `queued: datetime = timezone.now()` as a default value, which is evaluated at import time. This means all instances created without an explicit `queued` share the same timestamp. Evidence: `class AssignmentSource: ... queued: datetime = timezone.now()` in `src/sentry/integrations/services/assignment_source.py`.

AssignmentSource.to_dict() includes a `datetime` (`queued`) and that dict is passed through Celery task kwargs (`assignment_source_dict`). A raw datetime in kwargs is not guaranteed to be JSON-serializable depending on Celery serializer configuration, risking runtime serialization errors. Evidence: `AssignmentSource.to_dict()` returns `asdict(self)` (includes `queued`), and `sync_group_assignee_outbound` enqueues `sync_assignee_outbound` with `"assignment_source_dict": assignment_source.to_dict() if assignment_source else None` in `src/sentry/integrations/utils/sync.py`.

queue_consumer.py uses Python’s built-in hash() for queue assignment, which is randomized per process (PYTHONHASHSEED), contradicting the stated guarantee that a group is “consistently assigned to the same queue”. Evidence: FixedQueuePool.get_queue_for_group: `return hash(group_key) % self.num_queues` while docstring says “consistent hashing”.

queue_consumer.py references `queue.ShutDown` in exception handlers, but this exception is not available in many Python versions/environments; this can break shutdown handling at runtime. Evidence: OrderedQueueWorker.run contains `except queue.ShutDown:` in two places.

queue_consumer.py calls `q.shutdown(immediate=False)` on `queue.Queue`, which is not present in many Python versions; this will raise AttributeError (caught/logged) and can leave worker threads blocked on `get()`, making shutdown unreliable. Evidence: FixedQueuePool.shutdown: `q.shutdown(immediate=False)`; OrderedQueueWorker.run blocks on `self.work_queue.get()` with no timeout.

OffsetTracker.mark_committed removes committed offsets from `all_offsets` but does not remove them from `outstanding`, leaving stale offsets and breaking the intended invariant (and potentially leaking memory). Evidence: mark_committed updates `self.all_offsets[partition] = {o for o in ... if o > offset}` but never prunes `self.outstanding[partition]`.
https://github.com/ai-code-review-evalua: Redis Lua script references an undefined variable `span_key`, which will cause a runtime error when the script executes. Evidence (src/sentry/scripts/spans/add-buffer.lua): `if not is_root_span and redis.call("zcard", span_key) > 0 then ... redis.call("zunionstore", set_key, 2, set_key, span_key) ... redis.call("unlink", span_key) end` — `span_key` is never defined in the shown diff.

OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key uses math.floor/ceil on the ordered field value, but organization audit logs order by `-datetime` (a datetime field). Applying floor/ceil to a datetime will raise TypeError at runtime when optimized pagination is enabled. Evidence: src/sentry/api/paginator.py `return int(math.floor(value) if self._is_asc(for_prev) else math.ceil(value))`; src/sentry/api/endpoints/organization_auditlogs.py uses `paginator_cls=OptimizedCursorPaginator` with `order_by="-datetime"`.

OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key uses `math` but the diff does not add an import for it; if paginator.py did not already import `math`, this will raise NameError at runtime. Evidence: added code in src/sentry/api/paginator.py calls `math.floor`/`math.ceil` with no corresponding import shown in the diff.

OptimizedCursorPaginator enables negative queryset slicing when `enable_advanced_features` and `cursor.offset < 0`, but Django QuerySet slicing does not support negative indices and will raise at runtime. Evidence: src/sentry/api/paginator.py `start_offset = cursor.offset` then `results = list(queryset[start_offset:stop])` under the negative-offset branch.

Base paginator get_result change can still pass a negative start offset when `cursor.is_prev` is true (it sets `start_offset = offset`), which will also trigger Django's negative-slice error if a negative offset cursor is provided. Evidence: src/sentry/api/paginator.py change: `start_offset = max(0, offset) if not cursor.is_prev else offset` then `queryset[start_offset:stop]`.

Unstable cache key: is_errors_query_for_error_upsampled_projects builds cache_key using Python's built-in hash() of a tuple (hash(tuple(sorted(snuba_params.project_ids)))). Python hash randomization makes this value process-dependent, so the same org+project_ids will generate different cache keys across workers, causing inconsistent cache hits/misses and undermining the intended caching behavior. Evidence: src/sentry/api/helpers/error_upsampling.py: cache_key = f"error_upsampling_eligible:{organization.id}:{hash(tuple(sorted(snuba_params.project_ids)))}"

Factories.create_usersocialauth behavior regression: the function removed the normalization of extra_data from None to {} but still passes extra_data directly into UserSocialAuth(..., extra_data=extra_data). Callers omitting extra_data will now persist NULL/None instead of an empty object, which can break code expecting a dict-like JSON payload. Evidence: src/sentry/testutils/factories.py BEFORE had `if extra_data is None: extra_data = {}`; AFTER constructs UserSocialAuth with extra_data=extra_data without defaulting.

_set_sample_rate_from_error_sampling incorrectly ignores valid 0/0.0 sample rates: it uses `if client_sample_rate:` which treats 0 as falsy and skips setting normalized_data['sample_rate'] even though 0 is a valid float value. Evidence: src/sentry/testutils/factories.py: `if client_sample_rate: ... normalized_data["sample_rate"] = float(client_sample_rate)`
https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/: Test bug: In TestService_CacheCheck("Should deny on explicit cache deny entry"), the comment says it is allowing access to prove the deny cache is checked first, but the cached permission is set to false: `s.permCache.Set(..., map[string]bool{"dashboards:uid:dash1": false})`. This mismatch can mask the intended validation because the cached permissions do not represent an allow case as described in the comment.
https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/pull: GitHubInstallation.dispatch now blocks fresh installs: after confirming no existing OrganizationIntegration, it tries to fetch an ACTIVE Integration by external_id and returns the generic error page if it does not exist. In a normal new install flow the Integration row may not exist yet, so this change can prevent progressing the pipeline. Evidence: `try: integration = Integration.objects.get(external_id=installation_id, status=ObjectStatus.ACTIVE) except Integration.DoesNotExist: return error(request, self.active_organization)` added after the `installations_exist` check.

build_repository_query no longer includes `fork:true`, changing search semantics and likely excluding forked repos from search results compared to previous behavior. Evidence: function changed from `return f"fork:true {account_type}:{name} {query}".encode()` to `return f"{account_type}:{name} {query}".encode()`.

GitHubInstallation.dispatch can raise KeyError/TypeError when reading `integration.metadata["sender"]["login"]` because it assumes `metadata` contains nested keys `sender` and `login` without guarding; this can 500 instead of returning the intended error page. Evidence: `pipeline.fetch_state("github_authenticated_user") != integration.metadata["sender"]["login"]`.
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pul: NPE risk in IdentityProviderStorageProvider.LoginFilter.getLoginPredicate(): the new predicate dereferences idp.getConfig().get(OrganizationModel.BROKER_PUBLIC) without checking for null config. Diff: server-spi/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/IdentityProviderStorageProvider.java adds `.and(idp -> idp.getOrganizationId() == null || Boolean.parseBoolean(idp.getConfig().get(OrganizationModel.BROKER_PUBLIC)))`. If an IdentityProviderModel implementation returns null from getConfig() (or config not initialized), this will throw at runtime when organizationId != null.

NPE risk in InfinispanIdentityProviderStorageProvider.registerIDPLoginInvalidationOnUpdate(): update() passes `idpById = getById(model.getInternalId())` which can be null; registerIDPLoginInvalidationOnUpdate then evaluates `Objects.equals(original.getOrganizationId(), updated.getOrganizationId())` when both predicates are true, dereferencing `original` without a null check. Diff: model/infinispan/.../InfinispanIdentityProviderStorageProvider.java adds `registerIDPLoginInvalidationOnUpdate(idpById, model);` and the helper method uses `original.getOrganizationId()`.
https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/:
SQL parsing/table extraction is non-functional at runtime: pkg/expr/sql/parser.go TablesList() now uses NewInMemoryDB().RunCommands(), but pkg/expr/sql/db.go implements RunCommands as a stub returning `errors.New("not implemented")`. Therefore TablesList() will always error and NewSQLCommand() will always treat SQL as invalid:
- parser.go: `ret, err := duckDB.RunCommands([]string{cmd})`
- db.go: `func (db *DB) RunCommands(...) (string, error) { return "", errors.New("not implemented") }`

SQL execution is non-functional at runtime: pkg/expr/sql_command.go Execute() now uses `db := sql.NewInMemoryDB()` and calls `db.QueryFramesInto(...)`, but pkg/expr/sql/db.go implements QueryFramesInto as a stub returning `errors.New("not implemented")`. Any SQL execution will fail:
- sql_command.go: `err := db.QueryFramesInto(gr.refID, gr.query, allFrames, frame)`
- db.go: `func (db *DB) QueryFramesInto(...) error { return errors.New("not implemented") }`

readWebAssetsFromCDN now uses http.DefaultClient instead of the package-level httpClient (diff: `response, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)` replacing `httpClient.Do(req)`), which is a concrete behavioral change and can break expected custom transport/timeout/proxy/instrumentation configuration.

Delete(): removed the DashboardUID-specific branch and now routes all non-ID deletes through dashboard_id/panel_id/org_id. In the diff, `} else if params.DashboardUID != "" { ... dashboard_uid = ? ... }` became `} else { ... WHERE dashboard_id = ? ... }`, which will break callers that provide DashboardUID (string) rather than DashboardID (numeric) and can delete the wrong rows or fail to delete intended rows.

cleanup_test.go createTestAnnotations(): uses `min(...)` in slice expressions (`newAnnotations[i:min(i+batchsize, len(newAnnotations))]` and similarly for tags) but no `min` helper is added in the diff and no import is shown that would provide it. Unless there is an existing `min` in this package (not evidenced here), this is a compile error introduced by the change.

cleanup.go Run(): creates a ticker (`time.NewTicker(time.Minute * 1)`) and returns on `ctx.Done()` without calling `ticker.Stop()`. This is a concrete resource leak in the shown code path (ticker not stopped) and is present in the diff.

pkg/apiserver/rest/dualwriter_mode1_test.go: TestMode1_Get no longer compiles because `p` is still passed to `NewDualWriter(...)` but the local declaration `p := prometheus.NewRegistry()` was removed in the diff (`dw := NewDualWriter(Mode1, ls, us, p)`).

pkg/apiserver/rest/dualwriter_mode3_test.go: multiple tests do not compile because they call `NewDualWriter(..., p)` with `p` undefined in scope (e.g., TestMode3_Create/List/Delete/DeleteCollection/Update). Only TestMode3_Get defines `p := prometheus.NewRegistry()`.

pkg/apiserver/rest/dualwriter_mode3.go Delete(): request-scoped logger is constructed as `log := d.Log.WithValues(...)` but the context is set with `d.Log` instead of `log` (`ctx = klog.NewContext(ctx, d.Log)`), dropping the intended per-request fields.

pkg/apiserver/rest/dualwriter_mode3.go Delete(): metrics label/value bug—on success it calls `d.recordStorageDuration(false, mode3Str, name, method, startStorage)` passing `name` where other calls pass `options.Kind`, corrupting the metric dimension/value mapping.

pkg/apiserver/rest/dualwriter_mode3.go Create(): on storage create error it records legacy duration instead of storage duration (`d.recordLegacyDuration(true, mode3Str, options.Kind, method, startStorage)` in the `d.Storage.Create` error path), misattributing storage failures/latency.

pkg/apiserver/rest/dualwriter_mode3.go Update(): on storage update error it records legacy duration instead of storage duration (`d.recordLegacyDuration(true, mode3Str, options.Kind, method, startStorage)` in the `d.Storage.Update` error path), misattributing storage failures/latency.

pkg/apiserver/rest/dualwriter_mode3.go DeleteCollection(): legacy async path records storage duration instead of legacy duration (`d.recordStorageDuration(err != nil, mode3Str, options.Kind, method, startLegacy)` after `d.Legacy.DeleteCollection(...)`), so legacy metrics are recorded to the wrong metric.
GrafanaGroupLoader.tsx: `severity` prop string is unterminated, causing a syntax/parse error. Diff shows `severity="error` without closing quote in the new error Alert block.

useAbilities.ts: `useIsGrafanaPromRuleEditable` references `ctx` but no `ctx` is defined/imported in the function/module, causing a runtime/compile ReferenceError. Diff shows `ctx.hasPermissionInMetadata(...)` used twice inside the new hook.

GrafanaGroupLoader.tsx: potential runtime crash due to missing optional chaining on `groups` and `rules`. Code uses `promResponse.data.groups.at(0)?.rules.map(...)`; if `groups` is undefined/null, `.at` throws; if `rules` is undefined, `.map` throws. This is directly visible in the diff.

FilterView.tsx: React list rendering regression—`GrafanaRuleListItem` is rendered in a map without a `key` prop after replacing `GrafanaRuleLoader` (which had `key={key}`). Diff shows the `key` prop removed in the grafana case.

Potential runtime crash: `runSplitQuery` and `runShardSplitQuery` now unconditionally call `datasource.applyTemplateVariables(...)` on each query (`.map((query) => datasource.applyTemplateVariables(query, request.scopedVars, request.filters))`). If `applyTemplateVariables` is not defined on the datasource instance at runtime (older implementations/mocks), this will throw `TypeError: datasource.applyTemplateVariables is not a function`. Evidence: added `.map((query) => datasource.applyTemplateVariables(...))` in both `querySplitting.ts` and `shardQuerySplitting.ts` diffs.

Postgres bug: anonstore.updateDevice() uses MySQL-style '?' placeholders unconditionally, so when the device limit is reached on Postgres, the UPDATE will fail. Evidence: pkg/services/anonymous/anonimpl/anonstore/database.go defines `const query = `UPDATE anon_device SET client_ip = ?, user_agent = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE device_id = ? AND updated_at BETWEEN ? AND ?`` while CreateOrUpdateDevice switches placeholder styles only for INSERT/UPSERT.

Data correctness regression: CreateOrUpdateDevice no longer normalizes zero CreatedAt (and doesn’t ensure UpdatedAt is set) before inserting; it now always writes `device.CreatedAt.UTC()` / `device.UpdatedAt.UTC()`, which will store year-0001 timestamps if callers don’t set them, breaking the previously documented constraint/NULL handling and expiration logic. Evidence: removed block that set `created = time.Now()` when `device.CreatedAt.IsZero()`; new code: `args := []any{..., device.CreatedAt.UTC(), device.UpdatedAt.UTC()}` in pkg/services/anonymous/anonimpl/anonstore/database.go.

Logic bug in device-limit update path: updateDevice filters by `updated_at BETWEEN ? AND ?` using bounds derived from the incoming `device.UpdatedAt`, not the DB value or current time. This can cause false ErrDeviceLimitReached even when the device_id exists (rowsAffected==0) if the stored updated_at is outside that caller-provided window. Evidence: pkg/services/anonymous/anonimpl/anonstore/database.go: `WHERE device_id = ? AND updated_at BETWEEN ? AND ?` with args `device.UpdatedAt.UTC().Add(-anonymousDeviceExpiration)` and `device.UpdatedAt.UTC().Add(time.Minute)`; and `if rowsAffected == 0 { return ErrDeviceLimitReached }`.
https://github.com/ai-code-review-evalua: FallbackLocaleList#[] can return a String locale (the passed-in `locale`) while other fallbacks are Symbols, causing mixed String/Symbol locale keys and potentially missed lookups. Evidence: `def [](locale) ... [locale, SiteSetting.default_locale.to_sym, :en].uniq.compact` (config/initializers/i18n.rb).

FallbackLocaleList#[] calls `SiteSetting.default_locale.to_sym` unconditionally; if `SiteSetting.default_locale` is nil, this will raise `NoMethodError`. Evidence: `[locale, SiteSetting.default_locale.to_sym, :en].uniq.compact` (config/initializers/i18n.rb).

FallbackLocaleList#ensure_loaded! calls `I18n.ensure_loaded!`, but this method is only added by a freedom patch; if that patch is not loaded before this initializer (or in an environment where it isn’t loaded), it will raise `NoMethodError`. Evidence: initializer calls `I18n.ensure_loaded! l` (config/initializers/i18n.rb) while the method is introduced in `lib/freedom_patches/translate_accelerator.rb` (`def ensure_loaded!(locale) ...`).

I18n.ensure_loaded!(locale) is not synchronized and performs a non-atomic check-then-act on shared `@loaded_locales`, while `load_locale` itself uses a mutex. This can lead to concurrent threads both calling `load_locale` for the same locale (duplicate work) because the include? check happens outside the mutex. Evidence: `def ensure_loaded!(locale) ... load_locale locale unless @loaded_locales.include?(locale)` (lib/freedom_patches/translate_accelerator.rb) and `LOAD_MUTEX.synchronize do ... return if @loaded_locales.include?(locale)` (existing load_locale).
https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/:
MetricsMiddleware can panic on nil requests: methods dereference req (req.Queries, req.PluginContext, req.Body) without nil guards. Evidence (diff):
```
for _, v := range req.Queries { ... } // req may be nil
...
req.PluginContext
len(req.Body)
```
In QueryData/CallResource/CheckHealth/CollectMetrics.
https://github.com/ai-code-review-evalua: EmbeddableHost before_validation can raise NoMethodError when host is nil because it unconditionally calls sub! on self.host, and there is no presence validation to prevent nil reaching the callback. Evidence: app/models/embeddable_host.rb `before_validation do; self.host.sub!(/^https?:\/\//, ''); self.host.sub!(/\/.*$/, ''); end` and only `validates_format_of :host` (no `validates_presence_of`).

Migration CreateEmbeddableHosts interpolates unescaped host strings directly into an INSERT SQL statement, so a host containing a quote will break the SQL and it is SQL-injection prone. Evidence: db/migrate/20150818190757_create_embeddable_hosts.rb `execute "INSERT INTO embeddable_hosts ... VALUES ('#{h}', #{category_id}, ... )"`.

Migration CreateEmbeddableHosts can crash when expected site_settings rows are missing because it blindly indexes `[0]` on the result of `execute(...)`. Evidence: `execute(...)[0]['id'].to_i` for embed_category lookup and `execute(...)[0]['value'].to_i` for uncategorized_category_id lookup without checking for empty results.

Admin::EmbeddableHostsController#update can raise NoMethodError when the record is not found because it passes nil into save_host, which dereferences `host`. Evidence: `host = EmbeddableHost.where(id: params[:id]).first; save_host(host)` and in save_host: `host.host = params[:embeddable_host][:host]`.

Admin::EmbeddableHostsController#destroy can raise NoMethodError when the record is not found because it calls destroy on nil. Evidence: `host = EmbeddableHost.where(id: params[:id]).first; host.destroy`.

Admin::EmbeddableHostsController#save_host can raise NoMethodError if params[:embeddable_host] is missing because it indexes into it without guarding. Evidence: `host.host = params[:embeddable_host][:host]` and `host.category_id = params[:embeddable_host][:category_id]`.

desktop/topic-post.scss: In the replies section, the light-theme color value was unintentionally changed when converting to dark-light-choose. It used to be `color: scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%);` but is now `color: dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 70%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 30%));` (diff hunk around `nav.post-controls` -> `.replies` -> `a { font-weight: bold; ... }`). This changes light-theme behavior (30% -> 70%) and contradicts the PR’s stated goal of only switching to `$secondary` for dark themes while preserving the light-theme value.

mobile/modal.scss: `.custom-message-length` light-theme color was changed from `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 70%)` to `dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 70%))` (diff hunk around `.custom-message-length`). This is a concrete light-theme regression (70% -> 30%) unrelated to the PR goal.

mobile/topic-post.scss: In `a.star h3`, the light-theme color was changed from `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 20%)` to `dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 50%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 50%))` (diff hunk around `a.star { h3 { ... } }`). This changes light-theme behavior (20% -> 50%) and is inconsistent with the PR’s general pattern of preserving the original light-theme lightness.

desktop/user.scss and mobile/user.scss: In the user menu/group member info section, `.name` light-theme color was changed from `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%)` to `dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 50%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 50%))` (diff hunks around `.name { display: inline-block; margin-top: 5px; ... }` in both files). This is a concrete light-theme behavior change (30% -> 50%) and likely unintended given the PR intent.

Admin group pagination totalPages is off-by-one when user_count is an exact multiple of limit. In app/assets/javascripts/admin/controllers/admin-group.js.es6: `totalPages` returns `Math.floor(user_count / limit) + 1`, so 100 users with limit 50 yields 3 pages instead of 2.

Admin group pagination showingLast is broken: `showingLast: Discourse.computed.propertyEqual("currentPage", "totalPages")` compares currentPage to the literal string "totalPages" rather than the computed totalPages value, so it will never become true and the Next control won’t disable correctly.

Admin group pagination next action can set offset to user_count (past the last valid page start), producing an empty page. In admin-group.js.es6: `offset = Math.min(offset + limit, user_count)` allows `offset == user_count`; findMembers then sends that offset to the server.

Group members page template now iterates `members` but the route sets the controller model to the Group and does not set a `members` property on the controller. In app/assets/javascripts/discourse/routes/group-members.js.es6: `controller.set("model", model); model.findMembers();` while app/assets/javascripts/discourse/templates/group/members.hbs changed from `{{#each m in model}}` to `{{#each m in members}}`, so the list will render nothing unless `members` is separately defined on the controller.

Admin group page template iterates `members` but the admin route/controller no longer sets `members` on the controller. In app/assets/javascripts/admin/routes/admin_group_route.js: `controller.set("model", model); model.findMembers();` while app/assets/javascripts/admin/templates/group.hbs uses `{{each member in members itemView="group-member"}}`; `findMembers` sets `members` on the model, not the controller, so the list won’t render.

Spec uses wrong HTTP verb for remove_member. Routes define `delete "members" => "groups#remove_member"` (config/routes.rb), but spec/controllers/admin/groups_controller_spec.rb calls `xhr :put, :remove_member, ...` in the 'cannot remove members from automatic groups' test, so it does not exercise the real endpoint/verb.

Client-side rejected email caching shows the wrong message: when an email is in `rejectedEmails`, the controller always returns `reason: I18n.t('user.email.invalid')` regardless of whether the server rejected it as `user.email.blocked` or `user.email.not_allowed`. Evidence: create_account_controller.js adds `rejectedEmails` and checks `if (this.get('rejectedEmails').contains(email)) { ... reason: I18n.t('user.email.invalid') }`, while the server-side validator adds `I18n.t(:'user.email.blocked')` / `I18n.t(:'user.email.not_allowed')`.

Email domain restriction matching is too permissive and can match substrings: `EmailValidator#email_in_restriction_setting?` builds `Regexp.new("@(#{domains})", true)` with no anchoring or domain boundary, so a setting like `good.com` will match `user@notgood.com` and `user@good.com.evil.com`. Evidence: lib/validators/email_validator.rb: `domains = setting.gsub('.', '\\.')` then `regexp = Regexp.new("@(#{domains})", true)` and `value =~ regexp`.

BlockedEmail matching is case-sensitive / not normalized: `BlockedEmail.should_block?` queries `BlockedEmail.where(email: email).first` and the model validates `uniqueness: true` without case-insensitivity, so `Test@Example.com` will not match a stored `test@example.com` and duplicates differing only by case can exist. Evidence: app/models/blocked_email.rb: `where(email: email)` and `validates :email, presence: true, uniqueness: true`.

Client/server key mismatch likely breaks rejectedEmails population: server returns `errors: user.errors.to_hash`, which in many Rails versions serializes to JSON with string keys (e.g., `{ "email": [...] }`), but the client checks `result.errors.email` (property access), which will be undefined if keys are strings, so the email won’t be pushed into `rejectedEmails`. Evidence: users_controller.rb adds `errors: user.errors.to_hash`; create_account_controller.js checks `if (result.errors && result.errors.email && result.values) { ... }`.

EmailValidator file is missing a trailing newline (explicitly shown by git as `\ No newline at end of file`), which can break tooling/linters in production CI. Evidence: diff for lib/validators/email_validator.rb ends with `\ No newline at end of file`.

Invalid vendor-prefixed property in new flexbox mixin: `@mixin align-items($alignment)` sets `-ms-align-items: $alignment;` but `-ms-align-items` is not a valid CSS property for IE10/11 flexbox (they use `-ms-flex-align`). Evidence in diff: `@mixin align-items($alignment) { ... -ms-flex-align: $alignment;  \n    -ms-align-items: $alignment; ... }`.

Incorrect legacy flexbox order mapping: `@mixin order($val)` assigns `$val` directly to `-webkit-box-ordinal-group` / `-moz-box-ordinal-group`, but those legacy properties are 1-based while modern `order` is 0-based, so the same value changes ordering semantics in legacy implementations. Evidence in diff: `@mixin order($val) { -webkit-box-ordinal-group: $val; -moz-box-ordinal-group: $val; ... order: $val; }` and usage `@include order(3);` in `header.scss`.

Serializer conditional include hook is misnamed: `def include_website_name` is defined, but ActiveModel::Serializer expects `include_website_name?` for conditional attribute inclusion. As a result, the intended conditional inclusion for `:website_name` will not be honored. Evidence: `attributes ... :website_name` plus `def include_website_name
  website.present?
end` in `app/serializers/user_serializer.rb`.

`website_name` can raise on invalid URLs because it rescues only the first `URI(...).host` parse, but later calls `URI(website.to_s).path` without rescue in multiple branches. If `website` is present but not a valid URI (e.g., missing scheme), serialization can 500. Evidence: `website_host = URI(website.to_s).host rescue nil` followed by `website_host + URI(website.to_s).path` (and similar) in `website_name`.

cook_method enum mapping is inverted vs DB default: migration adds posts.cook_method with default: 1, while Post.cook_methods is Enum.new(:regular, :raw_html). That makes the default value (1) map to :raw_html, so all existing/new posts will default to raw_html and Post#cook will return raw (skipping cooking) unexpectedly. Evidence: db/migrate/20131219203905_add_cook_method_to_posts.rb `add_column :posts, :cook_method, :integer, default: 1, null: false` and app/models/post.rb `Enum.new(:regular, :raw_html)` plus Post#cook `return raw if cook_method == Post.cook_methods[:raw_html]`.

EmbedController spec mismatch with implementation: controller enqueues `:retrieve_topic` job when no topic_id is found, but the spec expects it to instantiate TopicRetriever and call `retrieve`. This will fail the test suite. Evidence: app/controllers/embed_controller.rb `Jobs.enqueue(:retrieve_topic, ...)` vs spec/controllers/embed_controller_spec.rb expecting `TopicRetriever.expects(:new)... retriever.expects(:retrieve)`.

embed.js can throw at runtime if the host page does not include an element with id `discourse-comments`: `comments` will be null and `comments.appendChild(iframe)` will raise. Evidence: app/assets/javascripts/embed.js `var comments = document.getElementById('discourse-comments')` then `comments.appendChild(iframe);` with no null check.

embed layout uses an invalid postMessage targetOrigin: it passes `request.referer` (a full URL including path/query) as the targetOrigin, but postMessage requires an origin (scheme + host + optional port). This can cause the resize message to be dropped and break iframe resizing. Evidence: app/views/layouts/embed.html.erb `parent.postMessage(..., '<%= request.referer %>');`.

Client-side max upload size is hardcoded to 10MB, ignoring per-type site settings. In `app/assets/javascripts/discourse/lib/utilities.js`, `var maxSizeKB = Discourse.SiteSettings['max_' + type + '_size_kb'];` was changed to `var maxSizeKB = 10 * 1024; // 10MB`, so the UI will misreport/enforce limits compared to configured settings.

413 error handler also hardcodes 10MB instead of using the configured setting. In `app/assets/javascripts/discourse/lib/utilities.js`, `var maxSizeKB = Discourse.SiteSettings.max_image_size_kb;` was changed to `var maxSizeKB = 10 * 1024; // 10 MB`, so the displayed limit on 413 responses will be wrong when the site setting differs.

`OptimizedImage.downsize` is defined twice with different signatures, so the later definition overwrites the earlier one. In `app/models/optimized_image.rb`, there is still a `def self.downsize(from, to, max_width, max_height, opts={}) ... end` and later another `def self.downsize(from, to, dimensions, opts={}) ... end`. Ruby will keep only the last definition, breaking any callers using the 4-arg (max_width/max_height) form.

`create_upload` can raise `NameError` for `content_type` on the API URL-upload path. In `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb`, the URL branch sets `tempfile` and `filename` but never assigns `content_type`, yet `Upload.create_for(..., content_type: content_type, ...)` is always called.

`create_upload` can raise `NoMethodError` when API URL download fails. In `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb`, `tempfile = FileHelper.download(...) rescue nil` can yield `nil`, but the code later calls `tempfile.size` unconditionally in `Upload.create_for(..., tempfile.size, ...)`.

TopicsController#unsubscribe can raise NoMethodError when no TopicUser row exists. In the diff, it does `tu = TopicUser.find_by(user_id: current_user.id, topic_id: params[:topic_id])` and then immediately dereferences `tu.notification_level` and calls `tu.save!` without a nil check/creation. (app/controllers/topics_controller.rb)

TopicUser.track_visit! assigns user_id incorrectly when `user` is not a User instance. The diff shows `user_id = user.is_a?(User) ? user.id : topic` (should be `: user`). This is a concrete logic bug that can write visits under the wrong user_id. (app/models/topic_user.rb)

Email template has an invalid class attribute value including a leading dot: `<h4 class='.previous-discussion'>...`. This will prevent CSS selectors expecting `previous-discussion` from matching. (app/views/email/notification.html.erb)
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/2: Cache delete UI does not invalidate/refetch connectedCalendars, so cacheUpdatedAt can remain non-null after deletion until a manual refresh. Evidence: CredentialActionsDropdown deleteCacheMutation only shows toasts + onSuccess callback; unlike disconnectMutation it does not call utils.viewer.calendars.connectedCalendars.invalidate(). connectedCalendarsHandler derives cacheUpdatedAt from CalendarCacheRepository.getCacheStatusByCredentialIds, so without invalidation the UI can show stale cache status.

Non-portable script: scripts/test-gcal-webhooks.sh uses `sed -i ''`, which is BSD/macOS-specific and fails on GNU sed (Linux). Evidence: `sed -i '' -E "s|^GOOGLE_WEBHOOK_URL=.*|GOOGLE_WEBHOOK_URL=$TUNNEL_URL|" "$ENV_FILE"` in the new script.

tRPC error contract bug: deleteCacheHandler throws a generic Error for missing/unauthorized credential, which will typically surface as an INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR instead of a proper NOT_FOUND/FORBIDDEN. Evidence: `if (!credential) { throw new Error("Credential not found or access denied"); }` in packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/calendars/deleteCache.handler.ts.
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/8: packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/slots.ts: `checkIfIsAvailable` compares two Dayjs instances with `===` (`if (dayjs(date.start).add(...) === dayjs(date.end).add(...))`), which is a reference comparison and will almost never be true. This breaks the intended `start == end` (all-day/zero-length override) logic branch.

packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/slots.ts: Working-hours bounds check computes `end` from `slotStartTime` again (copy/paste bug): `const end = slotStartTime.hour() * 60 + slotStartTime.minute();`. It should use the slot end (e.g., `slotEndTime`) to correctly reject slots that extend past `workingHour.endTime`.
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/1: deleteMany OR condition is not scoped to SMS for the retryCount branch, so it will delete ANY WorkflowReminder with retryCount > 1 (including non-SMS and future reminders). Evidence in diff: prisma.workflowReminder.deleteMany({ where: { OR: [ { method: WorkflowMethods.SMS, scheduledDate: { lte: dayjs().toISOString() } }, { retryCount: { gt: 1 } } ] } });

Retry policy is inconsistent/off-by-one: reminders are deleted when retryCount > 1 (i.e., at 2), while previous logic allowed retries until retryCount < 3 (stop after 3 failures). This change will prematurely delete reminders after only 2 failures. Evidence: deleteMany uses { retryCount: { gt: 1 } } and diff shows retryCount is incremented on failure; BEFORE snippet shows retryCount: { lt: 3 } with comment 'failed 3 times'.

retryCount increment uses non-atomic read-modify-write (retryCount: reminder.retryCount + 1), which can lose increments under concurrent runs/duplicate processing. Evidence in diff: prisma.workflowReminder.update({ data: { retryCount: reminder.retryCount + 1 } }) in both the else branch and catch block.
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/2: Org-scope authorization regression: memberships are only fetched when there are child teams (teamsFromOrg.length > 0). If an org has no child teams, userIdsFromOrg becomes [], so the non-team (personal) bookings branch is never added, excluding personal bookings for org members. Evidence in insightsBooking.ts: `const teamIds = [options.orgId, ...teamsFromOrg.map((t) => t.id)];` then `const userIdsFromOrg = teamsFromOrg.length > 0 ? (await MembershipRepository.findAllByTeamIds({ teamIds, select: { userId: true } })).map((m) => m.userId) : [];` and later only adds the personal-bookings condition if `userIdsFromOrg.length > 0`.
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/1: Zoho Bigin redirect URI is hard-coded to `/api/integrations/zoho-bigin/callback` instead of using `appConfig.slug`, which will break OAuth if the configured slug differs. Evidence: packages/app-store/zoho-bigin/api/add.ts changed from `WEBAPP_URL + `/api/integrations/${appConfig.slug}/callback`` to `WEBAPP_URL + `/api/integrations/zoho-bigin/callback``.

GoogleCalendarService persists the wrong value to `credential.key`: `parseRefreshTokenResponse(...)` returns a Zod safeParse result object, but the code stores that entire object as `key` in Prisma. Evidence: packages/app-store/googlecalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts `const key = parseRefreshTokenResponse(googleCredentials, googleCredentialSchema); await prisma.credential.update({ data: { key } });` and packages/app-store/_utils/oauth/parseRefreshTokenResponse.ts `return refreshTokenResponse;` (safeParse result).

parseRefreshTokenResponse’s `minimumTokenResponseSchema` is invalid Zod usage: computed keys like `[z.string().toString()]` create a literal key (e.g. "[object Object]") rather than validating arbitrary keys, so parsing will not work as intended. Evidence: packages/app-store/_utils/oauth/parseRefreshTokenResponse.ts defines `z.object({ access_token: z.string(), [z.string().toString()]: z.number(), [z.string().optional().toString()]: z.unknown().optional() })`.

Zoho Bigin CalendarService passes `credentialId` as the `userId` argument to `refreshOAuthTokens`, causing the sync endpoint to receive the wrong `calcomUserId` (credential id instead of user id) when the sync path is enabled. Evidence: packages/app-store/zoho-bigin/lib/CalendarService.ts calls `refreshOAuthTokens(..., "zoho-bigin", credentialId)` while refreshOAuthTokens expects `userId: number | null` and sends it as `calcomUserId`.

Salesforce refresh flow uses `response.statusText !== "OK"` to detect errors, which is unreliable and can misclassify successful responses (statusText may vary/empty). Evidence: packages/app-store/salesforce/lib/CalendarService.ts `if (response.statusText !== "OK") throw new HttpError(...)`.
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/7: scheduleEmailReminders.ts cancels SendGrid scheduled sends using reminder.referenceId without null-check. In the new cancellation loop it always calls client.request with body { batch_id: reminder.referenceId, status: "cancel" } even though referenceId is typed as possibly null (WorkflowReminder.referenceId can be null). This can send an invalid batch_id (null) to SendGrid and fail at runtime. Evidence: packages/features/ee/workflows/api/scheduleEmailReminders.ts added loop over remindersToCancel -> batch_id: reminder.referenceId.

deleteScheduledEmailReminder(immediateDelete=true) cancels the SendGrid batch but does not delete or update the WorkflowReminder row, leaving stale/orphaned reminders in DB. Evidence: packages/features/ee/workflows/lib/reminders/emailReminderManager.ts: if (immediateDelete) { await client.request(...); return; } with no prisma delete/update; and call sites pass immediateDelete=true (e.g., handleNewBooking.ts and viewer/workflows.tsx).

Multiple call sites fire deleteScheduledEmailReminder/deleteScheduledSMSReminder without awaiting the returned promises, so the surrounding handler/mutation can complete before cancellations/deletes finish and any rejection becomes an unhandled promise rejection. Evidence in diff: handleCancelBooking.ts uses forEach and calls deleteScheduledEmailReminder(reminder.id, ...) / deleteScheduledSMSReminder(reminder.id, ...) with no await; handleNewBooking.ts same; viewer/bookings.tsx same; viewer/workflows.tsx same, plus remindersToUpdate.forEach(async ...) is not awaited.
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/1: 2FA setup API can crash if CALENDSO_ENCRYPTION_KEY is missing: /api/auth/two-factor/totp/setup.ts now calls symmetricEncrypt(JSON.stringify(backupCodes), process.env.CALENDSO_ENCRYPTION_KEY) without checking the env var exists, so an undefined key can throw and break 2FA setup. Evidence: apps/web/pages/api/auth/two-factor/totp/setup.ts data.backupCodes and data.twoFactorSecret both use process.env.CALENDSO_ENCRYPTION_KEY with no guard.

requestReschedule.handler: destinationCalendar fallback typo results in always-empty destinationCalendar when booking has none. In packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings/requestReschedule.handler.ts:

destinationCalendar: bookingToReschedule?.destinationCalendar
  ? [bookingToReschedule?.destinationCalendar]
  : [],

This replaces the previous fallback behavior and (per the diff context) is a copy/paste error from the earlier code shown in the prompt (A || A). As implemented in the diff, it never considers user.destinationCalendar (unlike other handlers updated in this PR), so reschedule flows lose destination calendar when booking.destinationCalendar is null.
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/8: Async `forEach` in reschedule implementations is fire-and-forget, so deletions are not awaited and errors won’t be caught by the surrounding `try/catch`. Verified in `packages/app-store/vital/lib/reschedule.ts` and `packages/app-store/wipemycalother/lib/reschedule.ts`: `bookingRefsFiltered.forEach(async (bookingRef) => { ... await getCalendar(...); return calendar?.deleteEvent(...); ... })`. `Array.forEach` does not await returned promises, so the reschedule flow can continue/finish before external calendar/video deletions complete, and rejections can become unhandled.

Async `forEach` in TRPC reschedule path is also not awaited, so the API can proceed/return while deletions are still running and failures are lost. Verified in `packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings.tsx`: `bookingRefsFiltered.forEach(async (bookingRef) => { ... const calendar = await getCalendar(...); return calendar?.deleteEvent(...); })`.

Breaking change not fully fixed: `getCalendar` is now async, but `packages/core/CalendarManager.ts` still stores `const calendar = getCalendar(credential);` without `await` inside `getCalendarCredentials`. This means `item.calendar` becomes a `Promise<Calendar|null>`; later code had to be patched to `await item.calendar`, but this is an inconsistent contract and can break other consumers of `getCalendarCredentials` expecting `calendar` to be a `Calendar|null` (the diff shows only one downstream fix in `getConnectedCalendars`).

Calendar type normalization regression: `_crm` suffix handling was removed from `getCalendar`. Verified in `packages/app-store/_utils/getCalendar.ts`: it still strips `_other_calendar` but no longer strips `_crm`, so credentials with types ending in `_crm` will now look up a non-existent key like `...crm` in `appStore` and be treated as unimplemented.
